mentions.note: Preferred housing-sector way to play the theme; historically rallies ahead of Fed rate cuts.
Actual buyers showing up, stock flirting with new highs.
Rallied again on housing recovery and consumer spending resilience; beneficiary of housing stock turnaround.
Greenland peace dividend benefits home improvement retailer; up $953 or 2.5% on tariff relief.
Housing-related retail frozen until interest rates fall; holding in charitable trust for rate-cut catalyst; expect winners if peace/Fed cuts materialize.
Up in rally; sole bright spot in home retail.
Down 8% to 52-week low despite 3% yield; Cramer waiting for lower mortgage rates before adding; regards as one of two most problematic positions in portfolio (with AT&T) due to ICE concerns.
New low list, down 2.4%; Cramer's two worst stocks (HD and Nike); signals housing repair demand collapsing.
Hit 2-year low yesterday but surged 5%+ today on ceasefire/rate-cut hopes. Housing still in worst transaction volume in decades; wait for persistent rate cuts before committing.

Rate-cut template stock since 2007 (present since Elmont, Long Island 1987); owns during rate cuts; provides portfolio balance against software/SaaS exposure; get rate cuts with Home Depot, not Intel.
Market's go-to rate-cut play now eviscerated as rate-cut hopes fade due to war-driven oil and hot CPI expectations; needs housing turnover which isn't materializing.
Cramer's admitted mistake: bought expecting Fed rate cuts under new chair Kevin Warsh, but rates rose instead (30-year Treasury >5%). Hedge backfired.
Retail bust from elevated interest rates; housing market weakness persists; desktop down significantly with no relief expected.
Weak housing market limits upside (SSS +0.6%); owned as Federal Reserve rate-cut hedge; held up better than expected but needs Fed cuts to rally meaningfully.

Caller Adam held since 2001, asking about buying more. Cramer: 3% yield is magical level for HD. Buy more at 20x earnings. Fed won't cut rates—higher discount rates make dividend yield more valuable in current environment.
